strands.title: Log Format Discovery: JSONL Structure Confirmed
strands.content: CYCLE 40 successfully read proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl with limit=5. File structure is CONFIRMED: alternating request/response JSONL pairs. REQUEST entries contain: type='request', timestamp, model, max_tokens, stream, system (with system_prompt metadata), messages (with message_count, role_counts, tool_use counts, byte sizes, thinking_bytes), total_request_bytes. RESPONSE entries contain: type='response_stream', timestamp, duration_ms, first_byte_ms, status_code, response_bytes, usage (input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens). This is raw Claude API traffic log, NOT a conversation transcript. CRITICAL INSIGHT: To reconstruct conversation turns for projection, must PAIR adjacent request/response entries. Each pair represents one agent-invocation cycle: user provides input, system routes to Claude, Claude responds with cached/non-cached tokens. FILE SIZE CONSTRAINT REMAINS: 62,325 tokens in proxy file means ~1000-2000+ request/response pairs. Chunked processing still required. NEXT ACTION: Design turn-reconstruction pipeline that pairs requests + responses to extract conversation semantics.

key_claims.text: proxy_20260302_024551.jsonl contains alternating request/response JSONL entries. Each entry is a complete JSON object on a single line. Entries are typed ('request' or 'response_stream').
REQUEST entries contain full message context: role_counts, tool_use_count, byte sizes, thinking_bytes. Complete system prompt and message array are included in each request.
RESPONSE entries contain usage metrics: input_tokens, cache_creation_input_tokens, cache_read_input_tokens, output_tokens. These enable token accounting and cache hit/miss analysis.
Request-response pairs can be grouped sequentially (request_i, response_i) to reconstruct each invocation. Timestamp ordering and indexes enable pair reconstruction.

strands.title: Data Provenance: Raw API Traffic Log from Claude Agent Session
strands.content: Log entries show: model='claude-opus-4-6', stream=true, system_prompt includes Claude Agent SDK instructions (15,348 bytes across 2 blocks). Timestamps are 2026-03-02 (future-dated, likely synthetic or test environment). Cache control enabled (ephemeral). This is NOT a conversation export or chat interface dump — it is raw HTTP traffic captured from Anthropic API. IMPLICATIONS: (1) Messages array in requests is complete conversation history up to that point, not just current turn. (2) Token counts are actual API consumption (input, cache creation, cache read, output). (3) 'role_counts' shows distribution of user/assistant turns in the full history, not just current turn. (4) File is comprehensive audit log of one agent session: baseline_run1. Session likely ran multiple turns with same system prompt and progressive message history. DATA QUALITY: High confidence in token counts, timestamps, and API metadata. Conversation content is the question — must inspect messages array.

key_claims.text: Log is raw API traffic from Anthropic Claude API. Model is claude-opus-4-6. System prompt is Claude Agent SDK instructions (15,348 bytes).
Timestamps show date 2026-03-02 (future or synthetic data). Session baseline_run1 is contained in this single log file spanning ~4-5 second duration in entries 1-5 shown.
Messages array in each REQUEST includes full conversation history to that point (user/assistant role_counts). This enables turn-by-turn context reconstruction.

strands.title: Turn Reconstruction Pipeline: Request-Response Pairing Strategy
strands.content: CYCLE 40 DESIGN: To reconstruct conversation turns for tensor projection, must implement turn-reconstruction layer. ALGORITHM: (1) Parse JSONL sequentially. Group consecutive (request, response_stream) pairs. (2) For each pair, extract: request.timestamp, request.messages[-1] (last message in array = current turn), request.model, request.system. response.usage, response.output_tokens. (3) From request.messages[-1], determine role (user vs assistant) and content. (4) TURN STRUCT: {turn_id, timestamp, speaker_role, message_content, model, system_context, input_tokens, output_tokens, cache_hit}. (5) Reconstruct full conversation by sequencing turns. (6) For projection: each turn is input to claim extraction → strand clustering → epistemic assignment. IMPLEMENTATION: Chunked JSONL parser yields raw entries. Pairing layer consumes entries, yields reconstructed turns. Projection layer consumes turns, yields strand updates. This layering enables progressive processing of 1000+ turn log.

key_claims.text: Turn reconstruction requires pairing adjacent request/response JSONL entries. Pair (request_i, response_stream_i) yields one turn. Sequential pairing reconstructs full conversation.
Last message in request.messages array is the current turn. Prior messages form conversation history. Roles alternate (user/assistant) or follow speaker pattern.
Chunked JSONL parsing + turn pairing + projection forms a 3-layer pipeline. Enables processing of arbitrarily large logs with progressive tensor updates.

strands.title: Project Implementation: Hamutay Repository Structure Confirmed
strands.content: CYCLE 41 ACTION: Checked /home/tony/projects/hamutay/ directory structure. Contains: README.md (documentation), docs/ (expanded documentation), main.py (entry point), pyproject.toml (Python project metadata). This is ACTIVE PYTHON PROJECT. CYCLE 42 ADVANCEMENT: pyproject.toml FULLY READ. Content extracted: name='hamutay', version='0.1.0', description='Add your description here' (placeholder), readme='README.md', requires-python='>=3.14', dependencies=[] (EMPTY). CRITICAL FINDING: (1) Python requirement is 3.14 (future/development version). (2) NO external dependencies declared. This is intentional: lightweight project uses only stdlib (json, pathlib, itertools, etc.). (3) Version 0.1.0 indicates early-stage MVP. (4) Project is 'greenfield' — main.py likely contains minimal scaffolding. IMPLICATION: Implementation has zero external dependency constraints. Can use json, csv, itertools, collections, pathlib, re, logging from stdlib exclusively. Chunked parser, turn reconstructor, claim extractor, projector can be pure Python. CYCLE 42 FURTHER ACTION: Created src/hamutay/ directory via Bash. This follows Python packaging conventions (source tree isolation). Indicates preparation for modular implementation: src/hamutay/__init__.py, src/hamutay/parser.py, src/hamutay/reconstructor.py, src/hamutay/projector.py.

key_claims.text: Hamutay project: name='hamutay', version='0.1.0', requires-python='>=3.14', dependencies=[] (empty).
Empty dependencies list means implementation uses only Python stdlib (json, pathlib, itertools, re, logging, collections). No pandas, numpy, or external libraries required.
Hamutay is the implementation environment for tensor projector and API log analysis pipeline. MVP code will be written in src/hamutay/ with modular structure.
Python 3.14 requirement indicates development/future environment. May be test or staged deployment. Stdlib-only approach ensures maximum compatibility.
src/hamutay/ directory created via Bash (turn 125-126). Follows Python packaging convention. Preparation for modular implementation with __init__.py, parser.py, reconstructor.py, projector.py.

strands.title: Tensor Projection: Claims, Strands, and Epistemic Assignment
strands.content: PRIOR TENSOR (Cycle 39-40) established: (1) Format is JSONL. (2) Field structure is discovered. (3) Chunked parsing is required. (4) Epistemic heuristics needed. CYCLE 40 ADVANCEMENT: Turn-reconstruction strategy is CLEAR. CYCLE 42 STATUS: Implementation environment is now CONFIRMED. Project structure (pyproject.toml, requires-python='>=3.14', dependencies=[]) is fully understood. TURN CONTENT EXTRACTION: Each turn has message_content (text from user or assistant). Extract claims using linguistic markers: 'I think', 'likely', 'certainly', 'seems', 'unclear', 'suggests', 'probably'. STRAND CLUSTERING: Claims about same topic (tensor projection, log parsing, epistemic assignment, hamutay project, etc.) cluster into strands. Each strand is thematic thread with associated claims. EPISTEMIC ASSIGNMENT: For each claim, assign (T, I, F) based on: (1) Linguistic markers (hedging = I, confident = T, negation = F). (2) Prior context (is claim consistent with prior assertions?). (3) Grounding (is claim supported by evidence in conversation?). STRAND PERSISTENCE: Track strands across turns. When new claim arrives, either: (a) Joins existing strand (incremental update), (b) Starts new strand (topic change), (c) Resolves open question in strand (epistemic shift from I to T or F). OUTPUT: Updated tensor with new/modified strands, claimed losses, open questions, next instructions. CYCLE 42 READINESS: pyproject.toml read complete. Dependency profile clear. Ready to proceed with main.py read and initial module scaffolding.

key_claims.text: Epistemic assignment heuristic: Hedging language (maybe, perhaps, unclear) → I. Confident assertion (certain, definitely, proven) → T. Negation or contrary evidence → F.
Strand persistence is critical. Same theme across multiple turns clusters into single strand. Strand state (claims, epistemic values, open questions) evolves as conversation progresses.
Tensor projection is incremental. Each turn updates prior tensor: adds claims, modifies epistemic values, clusters strands, resolves open questions. Not a reset — an integration.

strands.title: Critical Path: Dependency Inspection Complete → Implementation Ready → Scaffolding Initiated
strands.content: DEPENDENCY CHAIN (from Cycle 39): Field structure → Chunked parser impl → Full log process → Claim extraction → Strand clustering → Epistemic assignment → Validation. STATUS AT CYCLE 40: (A) Field structure: ✓ CONFIRMED. (B) Chunked parser: ✓ UNBLOCKED. (C) Turn reconstruction: ✓ UNBLOCKED. (D) Claim extraction: ✓ READY. (E) Strand clustering: ✓ READY. (F) Epistemic assignment: ✓ READY. (G) Validation: ⧖ DEFERRED. STATUS AT CYCLE 41: (H) Implementation environment: ✓ LOCATED (Hamutay project). (I) Dependency inspection: ⧖ IN PROGRESS. (J) Codebase inspection: ⧖ PENDING. STATUS AT CYCLE 42: ✓ CRITICAL GATE CLEARED. (A) pyproject.toml fully read. (B) Project name: hamutay, version 0.1.0, Python >=3.14, no external dependencies. (C) src/hamutay/ directory created. (D) Implementation now UNBLOCKED. NEXT STEPS (Cycle 43): (1) Read main.py to understand existing scaffolding (if any). (2) Read README.md to confirm project scope. (3) Implement chunked JSONL parser in src/hamutay/parser.py. (4) Implement turn reconstructor in src/hamutay/reconstructor.py. (5) Implement claim extractor in src/hamutay/extractor.py. (6) Implement strand projector in src/hamutay/projector.py. (7) Wire entry point in main.py. (8) Test on first 10 turns of baseline_run1. BOTTLENECK ELIMINATED: No external dependency constraints. Pure Python stdlib implementation. Can proceed with MVP (250 lines) immediately in next cycle.

key_claims.text: Cycle 42 clears critical dependency inspection gate. pyproject.toml reveals: no external dependencies, Python >=3.14 (stdlib-only implementation possible).
MVP scope: chunked parser (50 lines) + turn reconstructor (40 lines) + claim extractor (60 lines) + projector (100 lines) = ~250 lines. Feasible in one cycle with pure Python.
Validation against hand-annotation is next critical gate. Must check: claim extraction recall, epistemic assignment accuracy, strand clustering coherence. Target >70% for MVP.
Cycle 42 has completed Hamutay project environment inspection (pyproject.toml). Implementation can commence in Cycle 43 after main.py and README.md reads.
src/hamutay/ directory successfully created. Indicates project structure for modular implementation is being prepared.
